findings.title: borrow_cost 0.0 suppressed by falsy guard before journal write
findings.why_it_matters: When borrow_rate_annual is explicitly 0 or hold_days is sub-second (both round borrow_cost to 0.0), the expression borrow_cost if borrow_cost else None evaluates 0.0 as falsy and passes None to _log_trade. The journal record stores None rather than 0.0. For tax/accounting consumers reading the journal JSON, None means borrow cost unknown rather than verified zero, potentially triggering downstream re-calculation or incorrect totals. The flag was meant to omit the field only when the short condition was not met, not when the computed cost is legitimately zero.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `borrow_cost=borrow_cost if borrow_cost else None` with `borrow_cost=borrow_cost if (pos_is_short and state.entry_price and getattr(state, "entry_time", None)) else None`. This passes the computed value (including 0.0) when the short-condition block ran, and None when it was skipped entirely.
evidence: Diff line 203: `borrow_cost=borrow_cost if borrow_cost else None` - Python evaluates 0.0 as falsy, so a legitimately-zero borrow cost becomes None in the journal.
Variable initialised as `borrow_cost: float = 0.0` at diff line 185; the calculation block runs only when pos_is_short, entry_price, and entry_time are all truthy.
Runtime verification: `python3 -c "borrow_cost = 0.0; print(repr(borrow_cost if borrow_cost else None))"` prints `None`.
Reproducing input: bot configured with borrow_rate_annual=0, or a short position covered within the same second it was entered (borrow_cost rounds to 0.00 via round(..., 2)).

findings.title: External-close path omits borrow_cost journal field for short positions
findings.why_it_matters: _detect_external_close handles the case where a broker stop-loss order fills without the bot calling _execute_exit — the most common exit path for short bots that rely on OTO stops. It calls _log_trade at line 96 with no borrow_cost argument. The journal entry for that exit will have borrow_cost=None regardless of how long the short was held. B5 goal is to record borrow cost for all short exits for tax/accounting; this path silently defeats it for the highest-risk exit scenario.
findings.suggested_fix: Add the same borrow cost calculation block (exits.py diff lines 184-192) before the _log_trade call at exits.py line 96. All required inputs are in scope: pos_is_short (parameter), state.entry_price, state.entry_time (new field), cfg.borrow_rate_annual. Wrap in the same try/except to preserve the existing error-tolerant pattern.
evidence: exits.py line 96: `br._log_trade(... broker=cfg.broker)` - no borrow_cost kwarg passed.
The diff adds borrow_cost only to the _execute_exit _log_trade call (diff lines 199-203); _detect_external_close is entirely unchanged.
_detect_external_close is invoked in _tick() when `not has_position and state.entry_price is not None` - the canonical detection path for externally-filled broker stops on short positions.

findings.title: entry_time not cleared in any position-reset block
findings.why_it_matters: All four position-state clear blocks in exits.py null entry_price, trail_peak, position_direction, and pending_close_order_id but none null entry_time. After any exit (normal, drawdown, or stale-mismatch), bots.json persists a stale entry_time. In the stale-mismatch path (line 272-281) entry_price is cleared without a subsequent _enter_position, leaving entry_time non-None. A future code path that checks entry_price before computing borrow cost would skip the calculation, but if the guard order were ever changed the stale timestamp would inflate hold_days arbitrarily. The minimal fix also makes the invariant explicit: entry_time is None if and only if entry_price is None.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `state.entry_time = None` alongside every `state.entry_price = None` in exits.py. Four locations: line 133 (_detect_external_close drawdown path), line 274 (stale-mismatch path in _execute_exit), line 400 (drawdown path in _execute_exit), and line 412 (normal exit path in _execute_exit).
evidence: Grep of exits.py shows `entry_price = None` at lines 133, 274, 400, 412 - none accompanied by `entry_time = None`.
Diff confirms entry_time is set in bot_runner.py _enter_position but no matching clear is added anywhere in exits.py.
bot_manager.py to_dict diff (line 159) serialises entry_time to bots.json; from_dict restores it via generic setattr loop, so a stale entry_time survives server restarts.

findings.title: Live spread may be negative when ask < bid (crossed market)
findings.why_it_matters: The guard `bid > 0 and ask > 0` does not prevent a crossed market (ask < bid), which some providers return transiently during open/close transitions or data hiccups. In that case spread_bps = (ask - bid) / mid * 1e4 is negative, and the frontend displays e.g. live spread: -3.2 bps. The field is informational only (not auto-applied to modeled_bps), so no backtest is corrupted, but the display is confusing and implies a guaranteed rebate.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the guard to `if bid > 0 and ask > bid:`. Crossed markets are then treated the same as zero quotes (live_spread_bps returns None).
evidence: Diff lines 258-262: `if bid > 0 and ask > 0: spread_bps = (ask - bid) / mid * 1e4` - no check that ask >= bid.
IBKR get_latest_quote (broker.py line 678) guards against NaN/zero but not ask < bid. Alpaca returns raw market data without crossed-market filtering.
Impact is display-only per B8 design intent (auto-apply deliberately deferred), so severity is P3.

residual_risks: borrow_rate_annual is not validated against negative values in BotConfig. A negative rate computes a negative borrow_cost, inflating short PnL in the journal. The backtest model (routes/backtest.py line 23) short-circuits at rate <= 0 returning 0.0, but the live bot path applies no such guard.

hold_days is measured from entry_time to datetime.now(timezone.utc) at the point the borrow block executes in _execute_exit, not from the actual broker fill time. The 3-second post-close polling window adds negligible error (~$0.0001 at 0.5%/yr on $10k) but the formula slightly over-counts hold duration for all short exits.
testing_gaps: No test for _detect_external_close with pos_is_short=True verifying borrow_cost in the logged journal record.
No test for borrow_rate_annual=0 verifying the journal field is 0.0 (not None) — would catch the falsy-guard bug.
No test for live spread endpoint with a mock provider returning ask < bid (crossed market).
No test for entry_time serialisation round-trip through bots.json across a restart for an open short, verifying hold_days calculation post-reload.
